summary: High‑energy first set and fiery Scarlet > Fire second set
blurb: The show bursts open with a punchy Far From Me and Deal, then launches into an iconic Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain sequence. Jerry’s guitar is in top form, Brent adds spark on his originals, and the drums/space segment fuels the night. China Doll and U.S. Blues close out a powerful outing.
review: April 9, 1987 at Chicago’s UIC Pavilion captured one of the Dead’s most exhilarating late‑summer nights. The first set builds relentless momentum – Greatest Story Ever Told segues into a thunderous The Promised Land, followed by tight renditions of Far From Me and Deal that showcase Jerry’s gregarious, high‑octane guitar work and Brent’s lively keyboards on his own songs. The second set is the real highlight: Scarlet Begonias flows seamlessly into Fire On The Mountain, launching a soaring jam that carries through Playing In The Band, Uncle John’s Band and an extended drums/space excursion before a soaring China Doll lifts the crowd into bliss. A concise encore of U.S. Blues rounds out a performance many reviewers call “one of the year’s best,” with Phil’s bass bomb at the end of The Promised Land and Bill’s youthful drumming adding extra drive.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of five recordings are marked positive, with consistent praise for the Scarlet > Fire sequence, China Doll, high‑energy first set and strong drum/space segment; only one mixed review notes muted highs but does not detract from overall enthusiasm.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape with clear instrument separation, strong crowd presence and consistent 'great' praise; captures the show’s energy better than the muddy matrix or fair‑quality soundboard sources.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain transition that drives the second set
Jerry’s blistering solos on Deal, Far From Me and China Doll
Extended drums/space segment delivering youthful energy
Brent Mydland’s lively contributions on his originals and tight keyboard work

band_performance.Jerry: Gregarious and energetic, tearing up his guitar with stellar solos on Deal and Far From Me; vocals strong on China Doll though occasionally not at peak.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation throughout, highlighted by a bomb‑like ending to The Promised Land that jolts the audience.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm guitar cuts through clearly in mixes where it shines, offering steady support and tasteful fills during the jams.
band_performance.Brent: Delivers confident, well‑placed keyboard parts on his original numbers and adds extra sparkle on Good Time Blues and Far From Me.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming is highlighted especially during the Rhythm Devils segment, described as goosebump‑inducing and adding drive to the set.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann’s ~15‑minute drum/space showcase brings youthful energy and propels the second‑set flow.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
